setting.location: France, 1917 — the Western Front, a muddy sector of trenches near the Somme
setting.description: The air in France is thick with suspicion. A small, isolated group finds itself bound together by circumstance — and by the disappearance that none of them will admit to witnessing.

crime.what_happened: A body was discovered in the locked study at midnight.
crime.initial_discovery: The housekeeper raised the alarm when she found the door sealed from within.

characters.name: Dr. Pemberton
characters.motive: Stands to inherit under the old will
characters.alibi: Claims to be in the library
characters.secret: Deeply in debt, creditors closing in

characters.name: Miss Crane
characters.secret: Was the victim's confidante and knows too much

evidence.name: Stopped pocket watch
evidence.description: Found on the victim, stopped at 11:47 PM

evidence.name: Torn letter
evidence.description: References a sum large enough to ruin a man

evidence.name: Muddy boots by the back door
evidence.description: No one admits to going outside

evidence.name: Empty medicine vial
evidence.description: An unusual sedative not prescribed to the victim

solution.method: Poison administered in the evening tea, timed to allow an apparent alibi
solution.motive: Stood to inherit under the old will; new will cuts him out entirely
solution.how_to_deduce: The stopped watch (E1) fixes time of death at 11:47 PM, contradicting Pemberton's alibi that he was in the library until midnight. The torn letter (E2) establishes his desperate motive. The medicine vial (E4) is a red herring — it belonged to Miss Crane legitimately.

key_twists: The will was changed three days before the murder
Miss Crane is a witness, not a suspect — she saw Pemberton leave the library early

_meta.setting_input: France, 1917 — the Western Front, a muddy sector of trenches near the Somme
